course.title: SQL Databases
course.description: Learn to store, query, and manipulate data.

chapter.title: Basic Queries
chapter.description: Introduction to querying databases with SELECT.

lessons.title: Selecting Data
lessons.explanation:
SQL (Structured Query Language) is used to communicate with databases.

Data in a relational database is stored in **Tables**, which consist of rows and columns. To retrieve data, you use the `SELECT` statement.

Note: For this interactive sandbox, we will create a temporary table for you in the starter code, so you have data to query!
lessons.analogy: A database table is just like a spreadsheet. `SELECT` is how you ask the spreadsheet to show you specific columns.
common_mistakes: Forgetting the semicolon at the end of the query.
best_practices: Capitalize SQL keywords (like SELECT, FROM) to make your queries easier to read.
lessons.summary: Use SELECT to retrieve data from a table.

exercises.statement: Write a query to SELECT all columns (*) from the 'users' table.
